name: Type-II seesaw
summary: Adds a heavy SU(2) scalar triplet Delta with hypercharge Y=1 that couples to lepton doublets. The triplet's small VEV v_Delta = mu * v^2 / M_Delta^2 generates Majorana masses for active neutrinos directly: m_nu = Y_Delta * v_Delta. Naturally tiny v_Delta from the seesaw structure of the triplet potential.

addresses_puzzles.note: Alternative to type-I; favored when the lightest seesaw scale is testable at colliders (M_Delta ~ TeV gives observable doubly-charged Higgs signatures).

touches_benchmarks.note: Doubly-charged scalar mediates LFV; mu -> 3e and mu-e conversion are saturated for low-scale type-II.

introduces.note: Triplet mass, trilinear coupling mu (lepton-number-violating), Yukawa matrix Y_Delta.

history: Magg-Wetterich (1980); Schechter-Valle (1980); Mohapatra-Senjanovic (1981) introduced triplet seesaw. Colider-scale variants studied since the 1990s; LHC searches for doubly-charged Higgs are direct probes.

references.citation: Schechter and Valle, 'Neutrino masses in SU(2) x U(1) theories', Phys. Rev. D 22, 2227 (1980)
Mohapatra and Senjanovic, 'Neutrino masses and mixings in gauge models with spontaneous parity violation', Phys. Rev. D 23, 165 (1981)
